what_it_does: Implements the `capability` MCP read tool: a single op-parameterized tool that projects the in-memory CapabilityRegistry (list/get/search/describe/snapshot), with lazy live discovery if the registry is empty, and exports the closed `OPS` set.
observations: Registers one MCP tool `capability` via `register(mcp, suite)` using `ToolAnnotations(posture="safe")`.
`_cap_registry` tries the module-level singleton first, then `suite.capability_registry`, and raises a teaching-loud `ValueError` if neither exists.
`_entry_id` strips a `cap://` prefix and builds `kind/name`, preserving `--` for flag names.
On first call, if the registry snapshot total is 0 and the suite has `capability_platform` and `discover_capabilities`, it triggers live discovery in place.
`op='describe'` returns a health view with counts by kind/posture and per-kind id lists.
`op='snapshot'` returns the raw registry projection dict.
`op='list'` and `op='search'` support filters by `kind`, `posture`, and `platform_id` and cap results with `limit`.
Uses `entry.model_dump()` to serialize registry entries, implying Pydantic-style objects.
standouts: Lazy live-binary discovery is triggered inside the tool invocation when the registry is still empty.
Error messages are explicitly framed as teaching/lane-wiring failures rather than silent empties or raw exceptions.
The closed op-set is exported as a contract-format constant (`OPS`) for machine inventory.
conventions: Follows the 'one parameterized tool with an `op` selector' design principle.
Exports `OPS` per CONTRACT-FORMAT §9.2 for contract-corpus extraction.
Uses type hints (`Literal`, `Optional`) and extensive docstrings.
Catches `RuntimeError`/`ImportError` broadly in `_cap_registry` before falling back.
Returns a tuple `(capability,)` from `register` for pkgutil discovery.
concerns: `_cap_registry` swallows `RuntimeError` and `ImportError` silently, which could hide real import problems.
The lazy-discovery branch returns a dict with `ok: False` instead of raising, diverging from the 'fail-loud' pattern used elsewhere.
Assumes registry objects expose `.snapshot()`, `.entries`, `__iter__`, `.get()`, and `.search()` without defining them here.
Parameter name `id` shadows the Python builtin.
Discovery is triggered on any empty registry, so a genuinely empty platform would still attempt a live spawn.
notes: A read-only MCP introspection tool that defers expensive live-binary discovery until first use and surfaces registry state through a closed, exported op-set.
questions: What is the exact class/interface of `CapabilityEntry` and the registry's `snapshot`/`entries`/`get`/`search` methods?
How does `suite.discover_capabilities()` ensure thread-safety when mutating the singleton in place?
Where are the five posture classification rules defined?
Is `OPS` consumed by `server.py` directly or only by an external contract corpus?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a single read tool projecting the binary-discovered CapabilityRegistry with the documented five ops and lazy discovery.
apparent_themes: single parameterized tool instead of tool explosion
intention_signals: To make the live Claude Code capability surface queryable through the MCP layer without hand-maintained lists, deferring the expensive binary spawn until an agent actually asks, while keeping the tool contract explicit and machine-inventoriable.
novelty: The tool call itself can bootstrap the registry by spawning live binary discovery on demand, and it exports its own closed op-set as a first-class contract artifact.
contribution: Provides a consolidated, safe, read-only MCP tool for querying the CapabilityRegistry, with an exported `OPS` constant, lazy discovery fallback, and explicit teaching-loud errors.
summary_for_embedding: This file registers a single op-parameterized MCP tool named `capability` that reads from an in-memory CapabilityRegistry, supports list/get/search/describe/snapshot operations, lazily triggers live binary discovery when empty, and exports the closed `OPS` set.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to expose the platform's live, binary-discovered capability surface through one safe read tool while keeping the op contract machine-extractable and avoiding silent failures.

symbols.does: Resolves the CapabilityRegistry from singleton or suite attribute, failing loud if unavailable.
symbols.description: Tries the module-level singleton `introspection.registry.capability_registry()` first; if that raises `RuntimeError` or is not importable, falls back to `suite.capability_registry`. Raises a `ValueError` with a teaching message if neither path yields a registry.

symbols.does: Normalizes a capability id from `id`, `kind`, and `name` parameters.
symbols.description: Strips a `cap://` prefix if present and constructs `kind/name`, preserving the `--` prefix for flag names. Raises `ValueError` if neither `id` nor both `kind` and `name` are supplied.

symbols.does: Registers the `capability` tool on the MCP server.
symbols.description: Called by `server.py`'s pkgutil discovery loop without editing server.py. Defines the nested `capability` tool function with safe posture annotations and returns it in a tuple.

symbols.does: The MCP tool that dispatches read operations against the CapabilityRegistry.
symbols.description: Validates `op` against `OPS`, resolves the registry, optionally triggers lazy live discovery if empty, and returns projections for list/get/search/describe/snapshot with filtering, limits, and teaching-loud errors.
